chunks.text: A: Yes, we are RERA-approved and DLD-approved valuers, accredited under IVSC standards. Q: Where is your office located. A: Office No. 324, Oud Metha Offices Bldg, Umm Hurair 2, Dubai, U. A. E. Q: How can I get a quotation. A: You can call us at +971 52 190 1127, email info@valuation. ae, WhatsApp us, or fill out the form on our website at https://valuation. ae/get-quotation/.

Q: What valuation methods do you use. A: We use sales comparison approach, cost approach, and income approach depending on the property type. For commercial properties, we primarily use the income approach. Q: What purposes do you provide valuations for. A: Golden Visa, property sales, mortgage and financing, taxation and financial reporting, legal purposes, insurance, portfolio management, feasibility studies, donations, and mergers and acquisitions. Q: Are you RERA approved.

A: We value all types of residential properties (apartments, villas, townhouses, land, buildings, compounds), commercial properties (offices, retail, warehouses, showrooms, factories, malls, schools, hospitals, labor camps), and mixed-use properties. Q: How long does a valuation take. A: Certificates are typically issued within 24-48 business hours. Q: Are your reports accepted by banks. A: Yes, our valuation reports are bank-accepted, RERA-compliant, and aligned with DLD guidelines.

=== WHY CHOOSE 34 REAL ESTATE VALUATION ===

=== VALUATION PURPOSES ===

The company provides valuations for various purposes:
- Golden Visa (Immigration)
- Selling Property
- Mortgage and Financing / Loan Security
- Taxation and Financial Reporting
- Legal Purpose / Court Cases & Litigation
- Insurance Coverage
- Portfolio Management
- Feasibility Studies and Development
- Valuation for the purpose of donation
- Mergers and Acquisitions

=== VALUATION PROCESS ===

Step 1: Preparation of Required Documents
- Request documents such as title deeds, architectural plans, legal permits, and any other documents related to the property
- Review documents to ensure accuracy and completeness

Step 2: Data Collection
- Conduct an on-site inspection of the property to assess its actual condition and gather necessary data
- Take detailed notes and photographs of the interior and exterior
- Verify the dimensions and areas mentioned in the documents

Step 3: Market Analysis
- Gather data on comparable properties in the area in terms of size, location, and features
- Analyze supply and demand
- Review sale and rental prices of similar properties

Step 4: Property Valuation
- Use valuation methods such as the sales comparison approach, cost approach, and income approach
- Estimate market value — determine the current market value of the property based on data and analysis

Step 5: Issuing Fair Valuation
- Write a comprehensive appraisal report that includes all details related to the property, the valuation methodology used, and the analyses and results
- Review the report to ensure information and valuations are accurate and correct
- Deliver the report to the client
- Discuss results, explain valuations, and answer any questions

Turnaround Time: Certificates issued in 24-48 business hours

=== ACCREDITATIONS AND COMPLIANCE ===

- RERA (Real Estate Regulatory Agency) approved
- DLD (Dubai Land Department) approved
- IVSC (International Valuation Standards Council) accredited — ensuring globally recognized credibility and compliance
- Compliant with international valuation standards
- Reports accepted by banks, legal institutions, and government bodies
- Bank-accepted valuation reports

Office Address: Office No.

Property Types:

- Serviced apartment complexes
- Waterfront developments
- Mixed communities

Valuation techniques: comparable transactions, discounted cash flow, and residual valuation techniques. Reports include tenant covenant analysis, service-charge apportionment, and practical recommendations to maximize value and marketability.

Method: Income approach to valuing commercial properties (as they are typically income-generating). In-depth analysis of total income, operating and maintenance expenses, occupancy/vacancy rates, and appropriate market rate of return. 3.
